verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is canonically a type II TM glycosyltransferase resident in the trans-Golgi, with its N-terminal TM domain anchoring the luminal catalytic domain in the Golgi stack. However, UniProt/GeneCards annotation (PubMed:1714903) explicitly notes a cell-surface PM pool ('mainly localized at the plasma membrane' in that specific study) where the processed cell-surface form acts as a recognition molecule in cell-cell and cell-matrix interactions including egg fertilization. On sperm, B4GALT1 is displayed on the outer surface and binds ZP3 glycans. The PM pool is real but secondary to the dominant Golgi compartment, and the processed cell-surface form arises from proteolytic cleavage of the stem region. This dual Golgi/PM localization, with the PM pool constitutively present in some cell types via its own TM anchor, qualifies as contextual/dual_localization.
